title: App Build Spec (v0.2)
$comment: v0.2 (specVersion 0.2) extends v0.1 from the P1.5 live calibration of home_banking (Core rev 6): attributes gain length/decimals/description/references(FK); entities gain isStatic/records/description; screens gain inputParameters/uiFlow; a minimal optional `integrations` block is added. NOTE: dataType enum stays camelCase (LongInteger etc.) while live ODC reports space-form ('Long Integer') — harness-verify's mcp executor normalizes; the spec's authored form is camel. The embedded example (examples[0]) is an ILLUSTRATIVE EXCERPT, not a complete spec: a single-screen excerpt whose navigation/navigates reference a 'transfer' screen it never defines, so it passes JSON-Schema validation but FAILS full harness-verify cross-ref. EXPECTED, not a bug. Do NOT 'complete' the example by adding the transfer screen. v0.2 ALSO gained (additive, backward-compatible — all optional): app-level `navigation` (shared nav authored ONCE as a block, not per screen), app-level `auth` (anonymous | app-local | platform-idp, with userEntity/adminAttribute/testUsers), per-screen `access` (adminOnly/requiresRole/redirectTo), and product-UI component types Modal/Tabs/Avatar/Badge/CommandPalette. Existing specs stay valid.
description: Contract between the spec factory and the build harness. The spec factory EMITS a document conforming to this. harness-verify VALIDATES a spec against this schema BEFORE building (validate-before-assert) — a spec that fails validation is a spec-gap wall up front, not a mid-build failure — then checks the built ODC app against each screen's acceptance assertions. Only describe what the ODC MCP can actually build: the dataType and componentType enums ARE the buildable surface.

roles.description: ODC end-user roles; screens reference these for access.

integrations.description: External systems/connectors. Optional. v1 is MINIMAL (REST only) per HD D3; home_banking uses none (context_connections is AI-model-only and returns empty). Widen as real apps need.

capabilities.description: The various ways and things a user can do (HD D11) — the user-flow layer. Each capability connects role(s) -> screen(s) -> action(s) -> entit(ies). Optional in JSON-schema, but REQUIRED for a complete spec; the harness-verify NO-HOLES check (--phase spec) enforces that every entity/screen/role is covered by a capability and every capability is fully pathed.

design.description: Design layer reference (HD D10). For a KNOWN app, the reference is the real product's captures; for a NOVEL app, generated (Figma). Per-screen visuals go in screen.mockupRef; this is the app-level theme/reference.

navigation.description: App-level SHARED navigation (sidebar/menu). First-class so the persistent nav is authored ONCE as a reusable block and referenced by every screen — NOT re-authored per screen (the per-screen path multiplies turn-cost + defect surface). Optional; when absent, nav is whatever screens declare via Sidebar/Navigation components.

auth.description: App-level authentication model. First-class so access control is describable + verifiable. `app-local` = identity in an app entity bridged via browser storage (no ODC end-user IdP; the common clone pattern); `platform-idp` = ODC end-user provider; `anonymous` = no login. Optional; absent ⇒ anonymous.

agents.description: App-level ODC AI Agents (v0.3, additive). Each is authored FROM SCRATCH via the `agent` recipe — internals + AgentFlow + BuildMessages + public Call service action + AIModel bound to a Trial AIModelConnection (MCP-only, wall lifted 2026-07-05). plan_from_spec emits one `agent` step per entry.

structures.description: App-level non-persistent Structures (Batch A, additive) — typed record shapes for action/agent signatures. plan_from_spec emits one `structure` step each, before the data model.

logic.description: Batch B: standalone logic units (service/client/SQL actions + global events). plan_from_spec emits one step each, last (a service action may wrap a write-path server action).

externalLibraries.description: Batch C: external .NET libraries (extlib_* lifecycle). plan_from_spec emits one `external-library` step each, first.

appReferences.description: Batch C: cross-app dependencies on producer apps' public elements. plan_from_spec emits one `app-reference` step each, early.

processes.description: Batch C: Business Processes (BPT). plan_from_spec emits one `workflow` step each, last.

odcDataType.description: ALLOW-LIST = buildable surface for attribute types. Complete/trim from the ODC catalog in the MCP doctrine notes. Anything outside this set is, by construction, a spec-gap.

odcComponentType.description: Buildable surface for screen widgets. Most are native ODC widgets (1:1). Board, Sidebar, Modal, Tabs, Avatar, Badge, and CommandPalette are COMPOSITE PRODUCT-UI PATTERNS (no single native widget) authored via a harness recipe and described by structured props (`board`/`nav`, or `props`). Extend from the ODC catalog + the product-UI primitive set as benchmarks demand (North Star: 100%-to-spec incl. real structure).

isStatic.description: Static (lookup) entity vs database entity. Live ODC reports this as `isStatic`.

records.description: Seed records for a static entity (one object per row, attribute-name -> value). Only meaningful when isStatic=true.

sampleData.description: Sample rows for a DATABASE entity (v0.3, additive), seeded via a LoadSampleData + WhenPublished timer by the `seed-entity` recipe. Used when the entity is rendered in a list but has no create UI (else its list is empty). One object per row, attribute-name -> value. Absent ⇒ the plan generates deterministic placeholder rows.

indexes.description: Batch B: entity indexes (plan emits one `entity-index` step each, after the data model).

length.description: Max length for Text; live ODC carries this per attribute.

references.description: Target entity name when this attribute is a foreign key. In live ODC, an FK is an entity-typed Identifier attribute (dataType '<Entity> Identifier') + a foreignKey object; in the spec, set dataType to 'Identifier' and `references` to the target entity. Preferred over the entity-level `relationship` block.

to.description: Target entity name (must exist in dataModel.entities).

id.description: Stable id; assertions and navigation reference it.

isDefault.description: Marks this screen as the app's default (landing) screen. Consumed by plan_from_spec's `screen` scaffold step. At most one screen may set it; if none do, the first screen is the default.

uiFlow.description: Owning UI flow, e.g. 'MainFlow' (role-gated) or 'Common' (Login/InvalidPermissions). Live ODC reports uiFlowName.

inputParameters.description: Typed screen input parameters (e.g. a detail screen's record Id).

roles.description: Subset of app.roles permitted to open this screen.

access.description: First-class access rule for this screen (complements/refines `roles`). Expresses admin-only gating + where to redirect an unauthorized user, so the role-gate is describable and the built OnReady gate is verifiable. Optional.

mockupRef.description: Path to the screen mockup asset. Drives ADVISORY visual diff only — NOT gating.

charts.description: Native OutSystemsCharts on this screen (v0.3, additive). Each is authored via the `chart` recipe (addReferenceToElements + SourceBlock + DataPoints from an aggregate). plan_from_spec emits one `chart` step per entry.

acceptance.description: The machine-checkable contract harness-verify evaluates against LIVE ODC state. Structural assertions are GATING.

aggregateJoin.description: Batch B: an aggregate join on this screen's list (see aggregateJoin def).

boundTo.description: Data binding: 'Entity.Attribute', an entity name, or an aggregate name. Verifiable against live state.

groupBy.description: PRODUCT-UI: attribute to group rows by (e.g. a List grouped by StateId → status section headers). The build authors group headers; the screen-walk verifier confirms it.

columns.description: PRODUCT-UI: rendered columns for a Table/List — expresses real rows (glyph/chip/avatar/identifier cells), not just raw fields.

board.description: PRODUCT-UI: for type Board — kanban columns + card spec (composite pattern, authored via recipe).

nav.description: PRODUCT-UI: for type Sidebar/Navigation — the app-shell nav items.

columnSpec.description: PRODUCT-UI: one rendered column/cell of a Table/List row.

kind.description: Cell render kind. glyph/chip/avatar/badge are product-UI cells needing an authoring recipe (CSS/SVG/IImage), not a raw value.

glyphSet.description: For kind=glyph: glyph family (e.g. 'workflowState', 'priority') → maps to the rendering recipe.

columnsBy.description: Attribute the columns group by (e.g. StateId → one column per WorkflowState).

title.description: Field rendered as the card title.

avatar.description: Field rendered as the assignee avatar.

toScreen.description: Target screen id (must exist in screens).

section.description: Sidebar section grouping, e.g. 'Workspace' or a team name.

appNavigation.description: The app's SHARED persistent navigation, authored once as a reusable block.

block.description: Name of the reusable nav block to author (e.g. 'SidebarNav'). One block, referenced by every screen.

workspaceLabel.description: Header/brand label shown at the top of the nav.

items.description: Nav entries; each item.toScreen must exist in screens.

showOn.description: Which screens render the nav. 'all' (default) or an explicit list of screen ids (e.g. exclude the login screen).

provider.description: anonymous = no login; app-local = identity in an app entity, session bridged via browser storage (no ODC end-user IdP — the common clone pattern); platform-idp = ODC end-user provider.

userEntity.description: For app-local: the entity holding users (must exist in dataModel). Its rows are the app's identities.

adminAttribute.description: For app-local: a Boolean attribute on userEntity marking admins (e.g. 'IsAdmin'); drives admin-only screen access.

loginScreen.description: Screen id of the login screen (must exist in screens).

sessionKeys.description: For app-local: the browser-storage keys the session bridge reads/writes.

testUsers.description: Seed identities to create per role so gated flows are exercisable (e.g. one Admin + one Member).

role.description: Role this test user holds (should be in app.roles, or 'Admin' when gated by adminAttribute).

isAdmin.description: Whether this seed user's adminAttribute is true.

adminOnly.description: Only admins (auth.adminAttribute true) may open this screen.

requiresRole.description: A single role (must be in app.roles) required to open the screen.

redirectTo.description: Screen id an unauthorized user is sent to (must exist in screens); e.g. the app home or the login screen.

does.description: Coarse step kinds. Refine against the ODC logic surface in the MCP doctrine notes as the verifier matures.

validate.description: Batch A: when true on a CreateEntity action, plan_from_spec emits an `input-validation` step after the create-form — a validation gate rejects an invalid submit (empty mandatory / bad format) before Save, so no malformed row is ever written.

guardExceptions.description: Batch A: when true on a CreateEntity action, plan_from_spec emits an `exception-handler` step guarding the Save<Entity>Record server action (OnException → graceful failure), resolving the flow's 'No Exception Handling' warning.

structure.description: A non-persistent Structure — a typed record shape for action / agent signatures (NOT a persisted entity; no identifier). Batch A: plan_from_spec emits one `structure` step each.

attributes.description: Typed fields of the structure (same attribute shape as an entity, minus the identifier).

toScreen.description: Target screen id (must exist in screens).

references.description: Target entity name if this input is an entity Identifier.

integration.description: An external system/connector. MINIMAL v1 (HD D3).

kind.description: v1 models REST only — the only connector kind home_banking would use. Widen (SOAP, SAP, …) as real apps need.

capability.description: One thing a user can do, end-to-end (HD D11). The connectivity spine: ties roles, screens, actions, and entities into a flow.

roles.description: Subset of app.roles who can do this.

screens.description: Screen ids this flow uses (must exist in screens).

entities.description: Entity names this flow reads/writes (must exist in dataModel).

actions.description: Action names this flow triggers (must exist on the referenced screens).

screen.description: Screen id this step happens on.

action.description: Action name invoked in this step.

source.description: Where the design comes from: a known product's captures, Figma, screenshots, or generated (novel app — a fresh modern design system synthesized by /design-layer).

themeReference.description: URL/path to the theme reference (palette, dark/light, typography).

references.description: Reference screenshot/source URLs or paths backing the design.

theme.description: Design tokens compiled into the app theme by the `theme` recipe (v0.3, additive). When present, plan_from_spec emits a `theme` step.

agent.description: An ODC AI Agent (v0.3). Built from a blank app_create kind=AIAgent shell entirely via MCP.

name.description: Agent name; a public Call<name> service action exposes it.

systemPrompt.description: System prompt, authored as the BuildMessages System message ContentText.

modelConnection.description: AIModelConnection asset to bind to the agent's AIModel slot. A Trial connection binds + publishes clean via MCP.

tools.description: Server Action names the agent may call as tools (each wired via a parameterless CreateActionHandler).

categoryField.description: Attribute rendered on the category axis.

series.description: One entry per series; each maps a valueField (attribute) to a DataPoint list.

sourceAggregate.description: Optional named aggregate to source DataPoints from.

themeTokens.description: Design tokens compiled into the app theme stylesheet by the `theme` recipe (v0.3).

fontFaces.description: Raw @font-face CSS for custom fonts (NOT @import — stripped at publish).

css.description: Additional raw CSS appended to the theme stylesheet.

assertion.description: A checkable predicate over live ODC state. These kinds define exactly what harness-verify must implement.

integration.description: Integration name (must exist in top-level integrations).

entityIndex.description: Batch B: an index over one or more entity attributes; unique rejects duplicates.

aggregateJoin.description: Batch B: a JOIN on a LIST screen's aggregate so it shows a related entity's display fields instead of a raw FK Id (NOT for detail screens — R2 cascade). plan_from_spec emits one `aggregate-join` step after the list-screen.

joinEntity.description: The related entity to join in.

joinAttr.description: The FK attribute on the list's primary entity that references joinEntity.

logicUnit.description: Batch B: a standalone logic unit. `kind` selects the recipe: serviceAction (public API) / clientAction (browser-side) / sqlAction (SQL node) / globalEvent. Kind-specific fields (inputs/outputs/wraps/statement/returns/purpose/payload) map directly to the recipe params.

externalLibrary.description: Batch C: an external .NET library. NOT a Mentor turn — plan_from_spec emits an `external-library` step describing the extlib_* MCP upload/publish lifecycle (needs .NET 8).

appReference.description: Batch C: a dependency on another app's PUBLIC elements. plan_from_spec emits an `app-reference` step (early, before consumers). Import EVERY touched entity incl. static — a hidden Id-only stub trips OS-APPS-40028.

producerApp.description: Producer app name or asset key.

processActivity.description: An automatic activity in a business process. Its ActionToTrigger can be ONLY a PUBLIC Service Action.

callsServiceAction.description: The PUBLIC Service Action this activity triggers.

process.description: Batch C: a Business Process (BPT). RUNTIME-PROVEN from scratch (wfprobe 2026-07-07): app_create kind=BusinessProcess does NOT auto-publish (0 deployments — safe window); one turn references the producer app's event + PUBLIC service actions AND authors the process; publish ONCE with the process present (a 0-process Workflow app publish corrupts its verify cache). plan_from_spec emits a `workflow` step LAST.

producerApp.description: The NORMAL (non-Workflow) producer app that holds the trigger Global Event + the PUBLIC Service Actions the activities call (CreateGlobalEvent throws in a Workflow app, so they live cross-app and are referenced).

triggerEvent.description: The Global Event that starts the process (StartProcessOn).
